subject: Constitutional Law
module: International Law and the European Union — Article 29

lessons.title: International Law — Article 29 and Dualism
lessons.description: Article 29 of Bunreacht na hÉireann governs Ireland's international relations.

lessons.title: Ireland and the EU — Article 29.4 and the Crotty Principle
lessons.description: Ireland joined the European Economic Community on 1 January 1973, alongside the United Kingdom and Denmark.

lessons.title: Primacy of EU Law — The Irish Position
lessons.description: To understand the Irish position, we must first understand what the Court of Justice of the European Union says about primacy.

lessons.title: The ECHR in Irish Law
lessons.description: Ireland was a founding member of the Council of Europe and ratified the European Convention on Human Rights in 1953. For five decades, however, the Convention was not part of Irish domestic law.
